refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating mission context into performance targets. This remains solution-neutral and avoids prescribing 'method,' 'document,' or 'history' which are design choices.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints from stakeholders), the system produces a traceable set of performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that satisfy those inputs. Verify traceability from worksite inputs to derived requirements.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall capture worksite mission context inputs (e.g., production targets, site constraints, safety policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from captured worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements with traceability to source mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.

candidate_dps: DP-NGHE-005.1: Establish a parameterized mission-context capture mechanism that ingests worksite operational inputs (safety plan directives, mission objectives, environmental constraints) and maps them to a standardized performance requirement template, with version control and traceability metadata.

DP-NGHE-005.2: Implement a rule-based inference engine that translates worksite mission context (safety policies, operational constraints, task definitions) into quantified performance targets via a declarative mapping layer, with audit trail of derivation logic.

DP-NGHE-005.3: Design a hierarchical performance requirement decomposition process that cascades worksite mission context through functional layers (system → subsystem → component), with explicit traceability links and baseline versioning at each level.

DP-NGHE-005.4: Create a federated data model that separates mission context acquisition (worksite inputs), performance target derivation (logical mapping), and baseline management (historical tracking) into independently maintainable logical domains with defined interfaces.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-NGHE-005.3: Design a hierarchical performance requirement decomposition process that cascades worksite mission context through functional layers (system → subsystem → component), with explicit traceability links and baseline versioning at each level.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between mission context capture and derivation logic if not separated by clear interface contract.
Dependency on external worksite safety plan and operational policy documents; changes to source documents must trigger re-derivation and version management.
Hierarchical decomposition may create circular dependencies if subsystem requirements feed back to system-level constraints without explicit governance.
Baseline versioning and history management could become tightly coupled to derivation logic if not abstracted as a separate concern.
coupling_check.notes: DP-NGHE-005.3 is preferred because it explicitly separates concerns: (1) mission context ingestion, (2) hierarchical decomposition logic, and (3) traceability/versioning. This supports independent evolution of each concern. Coupling risks are manageable through clear interface definitions between layers and explicit version control discipline. DP-NGHE-005.2 (rule-based inference) risks opacity in derivation logic and harder auditability. DP-NGHE-005.1 (parameterized template) may be too rigid for diverse worksite contexts. DP-NGHE-005.4 (federated model) is more complex and introduces additional integration points.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in a structured or semi-structured form (safety plan, operational policies) that can be parsed or manually ingested. RISK: If context is informal or distributed across multiple documents, derivation becomes error-prone and non-repeatable.

ASSUMPTION: Performance requirement hierarchy is well-defined and stable across different worksite types. RISK: If worksite contexts are highly heterogeneous, a single decomposition structure may not fit all cases, requiring customization per worksite.

ASSUMPTION: Traceability metadata (links between mission context inputs and derived targets) can be maintained without manual overhead. RISK: If traceability is manual, it will degrade over time as requirements evolve.

ASSUMPTION: Baseline and calibration updates are infrequent and triggered by explicit events (policy change, mission redefinition). RISK: If updates are continuous or ad-hoc, version control and history management become burdensome.

ASSUMPTION: Acceptance criteria require documented performance targets and history; no real-time dynamic re-derivation is needed. RISK: If worksite context changes frequently during operations, static baseline approach may become obsolete.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005 (revised): The NGHE solution shall provide a hierarchical performance requirement derivation capability that: (1) ingests worksite mission context (safety plan, operational policies, task definitions) via a defined input interface; (2) decomposes mission context through system/subsystem/component functional layers using a standardized mapping logic; (3) produces a documented set of performance targets with explicit traceability to source mission context inputs; (4) maintains a versioned baseline history with change audit trail; and (5) supports re-derivation and baseline updates triggered by documented changes to mission context. Acceptance criteria: (a) All performance targets are traceable to at least one mission context input with documented derivation rationale; (b) Baseline history includes timestamp, version identifier, and change justification; (c) Re-derivation process is repeatable and produces consistent results; (d) Traceability and version metadata are machine-readable and queryable.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level requirements decomposition, hierarchical performance baseline management, and traceability governance across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context and worksite operational parameters that seed the hierarchical decomposition process

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports performance requirement baselines and traceability metadata at each decomposition level for monitoring and validation

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional flow: receives safety constraints that influence requirement decomposition; exports safety-critical performance baselines to Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Distributes decomposed requirements and baseline versions to Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems for implementation

module_containment_scores.notes: Hierarchical decomposition and baseline versioning are inherently system-level governance functions. NGHE Solution owns the orchestration of mission context cascading, traceability link maintenance, and version control across all subsystems. Minor diffusion: subsystems must implement the decomposed requirements, but the decomposition logic itself remains centralized.

Vehicle is a subsystem that receives decomposed requirements, not the owner of the decomposition process. Placing hierarchical decomposition here would fragment the system-level view and duplicate logic across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. High diffusion: decomposition must reference mission context, safety constraints, and teleoperation capabilities—all external to Vehicle.

Teleoperation is a subsystem focused on remote control and operator interface. Hierarchical performance decomposition is orthogonal to teleoperation-specific concerns. Placing this DP here would create artificial coupling between operator control logic and system-level requirement governance. High diffusion: decomposition must span Vehicle and Safety Services, which are outside Teleoperation's scope.

Safety Services owns safety-critical requirement enforcement but not the broader hierarchical decomposition process. Safety constraints are inputs to decomposition, not the decomposition engine itself. Placing this DP here would conflate safety requirement validation with system-level performance baseline management. High diffusion: decomposition must cascade across non-safety subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The hierarchical performance requirement decomposition process is a system-level governance function that must maintain a unified view of mission context, functional layers, and traceability across all subsystems. NGHE Solution is the natural owner because it orchestrates the cascade from worksite mission context through system → subsystem → component levels, manages baseline versioning, and maintains explicit traceability links. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consume the decomposed requirements but do not own the decomposition logic itself. This separation of concerns ensures that requirement changes propagate consistently and that the system-level view remains authoritative.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic requirements repository without clear interfaces, subsystems may struggle to query or validate their decomposed requirements. Mitigation: Define explicit query and validation interfaces (e.g., via Tasking and Telemetry) that allow subsystems to retrieve their requirement baselines without direct coupling.

Risk: Baseline versioning and traceability link maintenance could become a bottleneck if NGHE Solution lacks efficient data structures or caching. Mitigation: Implement versioning as a lightweight, queryable artifact store with clear change notification mechanisms to subsystems.

Risk: Mission context changes may require rapid re-decomposition, but if NGHE Solution is not designed for incremental updates, the entire hierarchy may need to be recalculated. Mitigation: Design the decomposition process to support delta-based updates and partial re-derivation.

Risk: Safety constraints (from SafetyAlert) and operational constraints (from Tasking) may conflict during decomposition, creating ambiguity in baseline assignment. Mitigation: Establish explicit conflict resolution rules and escalation paths within NGHE Solution's decomposition logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to DP choice is necessary. DP-NGHE-005.3 is appropriately scoped as a system-level governance function and maps cleanly to NGHE Solution. However, to strengthen containment and reduce diffusion, recommend refining the DP to explicitly include: (1) a requirement query interface that subsystems use to retrieve their decomposed baselines, (2) a change notification mechanism that propagates baseline updates to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and (3) a conflict resolution protocol for reconciling mission context, safety constraints, and operational constraints during decomposition. This ensures NGHE Solution remains the authoritative owner while providing clear, non-diffusive interfaces to subsystems.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-NGHE-005.3 directly addresses FR-NGHE-005 by specifying a hierarchical decomposition process that cascades worksite mission context through functional layers with explicit traceability and baseline versioning. The DP is solution-agnostic and logically sound: it defines the mechanism (hierarchical cascade + traceability + versioning) without prescribing implementation technology. The DP is verifiable against the acceptance criterion 'produces a documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintains a history of baselines/calibration updates.'

diffusion_risks: Minor diffusion (0.08 score) is acceptable and well-characterized. Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must implement decomposed requirements, but this is consumption, not ownership of the decomposition logic itself.

Risk: Requirement query interface (mentioned in recommended_revision) is not yet explicitly designed. If subsystems must directly access NGHE Solution's internal data structures to retrieve their baselines, coupling will increase and diffusion will rise. Mitigation: Formalize a read-only query interface as part of DP refinement.

Risk: Change notification mechanism is implicit in the DP but not explicitly specified. If baseline updates are not actively propagated to subsystems, they may operate on stale requirements. Mitigation: Add explicit change notification as a DP sub-component.

Risk: Conflict resolution protocol (safety vs. operational constraints) is mentioned as a containment risk but not yet part of the DP specification. If conflicts are resolved ad-hoc or outside NGHE Solution, decomposition authority becomes diffuse. Mitigation: Elevate conflict resolution to explicit DP responsibility.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct owner with high containment (0.92) and low diffusion (0.08). The hierarchical decomposition process is inherently a system-level governance function that must maintain a unified view across all subsystems. Subsystems are correctly positioned as consumers of decomposed requirements, not owners of decomposition logic. The module boundary is well-defined: NGHE Solution orchestrates mission context cascading, baseline versioning, and traceability governance. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are appropriate and non-diffusive. However, to strengthen containment further, recommend formalizing the query interface, change notification mechanism, and conflict resolution protocol as explicit DP sub-components or interface specifications.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The acceptance criterion 'produces a documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintains a history of baselines/calibration updates' is directly verifiable through NGHE Solution's outputs. Verification approach: (1) Inspect decomposed requirement artifacts for traceability links back to worksite mission context (Tasking input), (2) Validate that baseline versioning is maintained and queryable via Telemetry interface, (3) Confirm that SafetyAlert constraints are reflected in safety-critical performance baselines, (4) Audit Network Link exports to verify that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services receive consistent, versioned requirement baselines. Worksite safety plan and operational policies can be traced as inputs to the decomposition process.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution via DP-NGHE-005.3 with explicit responsibility for hierarchical decomposition, baseline management, and traceability governance. Subsystem responsibilities are well-defined: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services receive and implement decomposed requirements but do not own the decomposition process. The allocation is non-ambiguous and supports clear ownership and accountability.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-NGHE-005.3 to explicitly include a requirement query interface specification that allows subsystems to retrieve their decomposed baselines without direct coupling to NGHE Solution's internal data structures. Define query semantics (e.g., by subsystem, by functional layer, by baseline version).

Add a change notification mechanism to DP-NGHE-005.3 that actively propagates baseline updates to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Specify notification triggers (e.g., mission context change, safety constraint update) and delivery guarantees (e.g., at-least-once, ordered).

Formalize a conflict resolution protocol within DP-NGHE-005.3 for reconciling mission context, safety constraints (from SafetyAlert), and operational constraints (from Tasking) during decomposition. Define escalation paths and decision authority (e.g., when conflicts cannot be automatically resolved).

Strengthen the DP specification to clarify the scope of 'baseline versioning': define what constitutes a version boundary (e.g., mission context change, safety constraint update, operator request), how versions are labeled and stored, and how subsystems query historical baselines.

Consider adding a DP sub-component or separate DP for requirement validation: after decomposition, NGHE Solution should validate that decomposed baselines are feasible and consistent with subsystem capabilities. This validation should feed back to the decomposition process to enable iterative refinement.

Document the interface contract between NGHE Solution and subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to clarify the format, frequency, and semantics of requirement baseline exports via Network Link. This will reduce ambiguity and support integration testing.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment/allocation are clear. However, the DP-IM gate identifies three critical diffusion risks that are unacceptable given the high safety criticality of this requirement. The risks are not fundamental flaws but rather gaps in DP specification that must be closed before proceeding to sub-FR derivation. Specifically: (1) the requirement query interface is implicit and risks coupling; (2) change notification is implicit and risks stale baselines in safety-critical subsystems; (3) conflict resolution protocol is absent and risks diffuse decomposition authority. These gaps directly threaten the integrity of the hierarchical decomposition process itself. Revising DP-NGHE-005.3 to explicitly formalize these three mechanisms will eliminate diffusion risks, strengthen containment, and provide a solid foundation for sub-FR derivation. The recommended revisions are surgical and do not invalidate the current DP logic—they operationalize it.

revise.why: DP-NGHE-005.3 must be refined to explicitly specify: (1) a read-only requirement query interface with defined semantics (by subsystem, by functional layer, by baseline version) to decouple subsystems from NGHE Solution's internal data structures; (2) an active change notification mechanism with explicit triggers (mission context change, safety constraint update) and delivery guarantees (at-least-once, ordered) to prevent subsystems from operating on stale baselines; (3) a formal conflict resolution protocol for reconciling mission context, safety constraints (SafetyAlert), and operational constraints (Tasking) during decomposition, with defined escalation paths and decision authority. Additionally, strengthen the DP to clarify baseline versioning scope (what constitutes a version boundary, labeling scheme, storage, historical query), add a requirement validation sub-component (feasibility and consistency checks post-decomposition), and document the interface contract for requirement baseline exports via Network Link. These refinements close the identified diffusion gaps and are essential given high safety criticality.

assumptions: NGHE Solution is the authoritative owner of hierarchical decomposition logic and baseline governance across all subsystems.
Subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are consumers of decomposed requirements and do not own decomposition logic.
Mission context cascading, baseline versioning, and traceability governance are system-level functions that must maintain unified governance.
Worksite mission context (Tasking input), safety constraints (SafetyAlert input), and operational policies are stable inputs to the decomposition process.
Subsystems can be decoupled from NGHE Solution's internal data structures via a well-defined query interface.
Change notification can be reliably delivered to subsystems with defined triggers and guarantees.
Conflict resolution authority can be formally assigned and escalation paths can be pre-defined.
open_risks: If the requirement query interface is not formalized, subsystems may directly couple to NGHE Solution's internal data structures, increasing diffusion and making future refactoring difficult.

If change notification is not actively implemented, subsystems may operate on stale baseline versions, creating safety-critical inconsistencies between decomposed requirements and actual system behavior.

If conflict resolution protocol is not formalized, conflicts between mission context, safety constraints, and operational constraints may be resolved ad-hoc or outside NGHE Solution, diffusing decomposition authority and creating traceability gaps.
If baseline versioning scope is not clearly defined, subsystems may be uncertain about what constitutes a version boundary, leading to inconsistent baseline consumption and potential safety gaps.

If requirement validation (feasibility and consistency checks) is not part of the DP, decomposed baselines may be infeasible or inconsistent with subsystem capabilities, requiring rework and delaying deployment.
If the interface contract for requirement baseline exports is not documented, integration testing may reveal ambiguities in format, frequency, or semantics, delaying subsystem integration.
evidence_needed: Refined DP-NGHE-005.3 specification with explicit query interface definition (semantics, access patterns, response formats).
Change notification mechanism specification with explicit triggers, delivery guarantees, and retry/ordering semantics.
Formal conflict resolution protocol with decision rules, escalation paths, and authority assignment.
Baseline versioning specification clarifying version boundaries, labeling scheme, storage model, and historical query interface.
Requirement validation sub-component specification defining feasibility checks, consistency rules, and feedback loops to decomposition.
Interface contract documentation for requirement baseline exports via Network Link (format, frequency, semantics, versioning).
Traceability matrix linking refined DP sub-components to diffusion risks identified in DP-IM gate.
Safety analysis confirming that formalized mechanisms (query interface, change notification, conflict resolution) do not introduce new failure modes or reduce safety margins.
